description: IPv4 CIDR bit-algebra modelled on the `netaddr` library (BSD; ~11.2M downloads/month). Curates the operations that are distinctively netaddr's and have NO stdlib `ipaddress` counterpart: `spanningCidr` (the single smallest aligned CIDR spanning a list of blocks) and `iprangeToCidrs` (the minimal aligned-block decomposition of an inclusive address range), plus the frozen mask anchors containsAddr/networkAddr/broadcast. Every spec is anchored on frozen Nat mask arithmetic (`/`, `*`, `%`, `^`, `==`, `≤`, `<`) over the full `∀ a < 2^32` address domain. `spanningCidr`'s headline set is aligned ∧ full-domain coverage (witness) ∧ MAXIMAL-PREFIX optimality (no aligned block of strictly longer prefix covers every input — kills the `0.0.0.0/0` passthrough), with coversAll/le-each/singleton supports. `iprangeToCidrs`'s headline set is exact two-sided coverage (`covered ↔ lo ≤ a ≤ hi`, full domain) ∧ all-aligned ∧ within-range ∧ NO-ADJACENT-MERGE minimality (no two consecutive outputs are mergeable siblings — kills the all-`/32` over-split), with disjointness, endpoint witnesses, the single-aligned-block fixpoint, and the empty/point edge cases. Maximal-prefix optimality is proved via a longest-common-prefix fold whose agreement invariant is downward-closed; the no-merge minimality hinges on the hidden parent-alignment lemma (a consecutive `/p` pair tiling a `/(p-1)` block forces the lower base aligned to `p-1`, so the greedy `maxBlockAt` would have emitted the bigger block). Deliberately DISTINCT from the sibling `ipaddress` benchmark (which curates `collapse_addresses`): neither spanningCidr nor iprangeToCidrs is a contained-block-removal pass, and there is no round-trip or oracle-copy. Scope: the pure 32-bit bit-algebra core only (no IANA/DNS/glob/nmap I/O, no cidr_merge — the sibling-coalescing merge overlaps ipaddress.collapse and is omitted in favour of the two distinct drivers).
